emergent_themes.description: Students who approach delivery with analytical precision, tracking spending, comparing platforms systematically, and optimizing for maximum value through strategic ordering patterns
Clear time-based decision points (e.g., 'if I'm home by 6pm I cook, otherwise I order') that create predictable ordering triggers
Students with significantly higher food budgets (£150-200/week) who use delivery strategically rather than being constrained by cost, enabled by part-time work or family support

coding_summary: Yash demonstrates a highly systematic and analytical approach to food delivery, treating it as a strategic tool for time management rather than an indulgence. His 50/50 split between cooking and ordering reflects careful optimization based on time availability, with clear decision thresholds (home by 6pm = cook, later = order). His high food budget (£150-200 weekly) enables promotion-driven platform switching and bulk ordering strategies. He shows strong post-graduation commitment to delivery services, expecting increased reliance due to full-time work demands. His detailed platform comparisons and subscription value calculations reveal sophisticated understanding of the delivery ecosystem.

coverage_gaps.reason: Participant shows no evidence of stigma around ordering - treats it as a practical tool without emotional conflict
While participant expects increased usage post-graduation, no specific discussion of how value perception might change with higher income
